question: 1) SUCCESS — I read docs/product/login-flow.md (SRD-014, AC-1): success = after entering the QA credentials and ticking the terms box, the app leaves the login screen and lands on the main screen with the bottom tab bar (Trang chu / Truyen hinh / Ca nhan). Is that still the assertion you want, or should it be tighter/looser (e.g. assert a specific tab is selected)?

2) FAILURE — SRD-014 section 5.1 / AC-2 says a wrong username or password keeps the user ON the login screen and shows a red inline message 'Sai ten dang nhap hoac mat khau' directly under the password field (no popup, no bounce back to the intro screen). Still true in the current build? And do you want the test to cover that negative case too, or happy path only?

3) BLOCKERS — the doc's main flow step 1 says iOS shows a notification-permission dialog on the first launch after install/data wipe, and reset_app_data is 'always' so the suite will hit it every run. Which action should the test (and I, while exploring) take on that dialog: Allow or Don't Allow? Also: the doc says the QA account has no OTP/2FA and staging is up — anything else that could block the run (paywall, feature flag, rate limit)?

4) ENTRY POINT — codegraph shows AppRouter.presentLogin is called from two places: IntroductionContentViewController.didTapLogin ('Dang nhap ngay' on the intro screen, cold start) and ProfileViewController.didTapSignIn ('Dang nhap de tiep tuc' on the Profile tab in guest mode). Which one should this test enter through — or both?

BLOCKER (observed live, not a guess): on launch after the data wipe the app is covered by a SpringBoard-owned alert -- '"MyTV" Would Like to Send You Notifications' with buttons 'Don't Allow' and 'Allow'. Because reset_app_data is 'always' this will fire on EVERY test run, so the test has to dismiss it in setup. Which button should I tap -- 'Allow' or 'Don't Allow'? I will use the same one while exploring and in the test setup.

FOLLOW-UP on a question from my batched round that did not get a direct answer (the replies came back shifted by one): what does a real FAILURE of this flow look like? Confirm or correct that, and tell me whether you want the test to cover the wrong-password negative case as well as the happy path.

The iOS notifications alert is on screen right now and I cannot continue until it is dismissed. Reply with exactly one word -- either 'Allow' or 'Deny' -- to tell me which button to tap. I will use the same one in the test's setup on every run.

HEADS UP -- the shipped build does not match SRD-014 AC-3. The doc (section 4 step 4 / AC-3) says the 'Dang nhap' button is DISABLED until the terms checkbox is ticked. Live, the button is enabled=true the whole time; the rule is instead enforced when you submit -- tapping it with the box unticked keeps you on the login screen and shows the inline text 'Vui long dong y voi dieu khoan su dung'. I am going to assert the live behaviour, not AC-3. Do you want me to file/flag this doc-vs-build gap anywhere?

I drove a deliberately wrong password against the live build and observed: the app STAYS on the login screen and renders an inline text element 'Sai ten dang nhap hoac mat khau'. No popup, no bounce back to the intro screen -- so SRD-014 section 5.1 still matches the build. Do you want me to include this wrong-password case as a second test alongside the happy path, or ship the happy path only for now?

1) SUCCESS — when does this test pass? I've mapped the code: LoginViewController.handle(result:) on success calls AppRouter.presentHome(guest:false), which swaps the root to MainTabBarController (tabs: Trang chủ / Truyền hình / Cá nhân). Is 'after logging in with the QA account, the app shows the main tab bar screen' the assertion you want, or do you want something more specific (e.g. a named element on Trang chủ, or the Cá nhân tab showing the account instead of the guest sign-in prompt)?

2) FAILURE — what does a real failure look like, so I never loosen the assertion to make it pass? Code shows loginView.showError("Sai tên đăng nhập hoặc mật khẩu") keeps you on the login screen with an inline red label (no popup, no bounce back to Introduction). Confirm that's the failure signature, and tell me whether you want the wrong-password case covered as a negative test in this same file or not at all.

3) BLOCKERS — what could stop this run without the feature being broken? Ones I can see from the code: (a) IntroductionContentViewController.viewDidLoad calls NotificationPermission.requestIfNeeded(), so a system notification permission alert likely fires on every launch (reset_app_data: always wipes the grant each run) — do you want the test to tap Allow or Don't Allow?; (b) the terms checkbox gates btnLogin.isEnabled, so the test must tick it; (c) OTP/2FA on the QA account — does it have any?; (d) anything else (backend/test-data/feature-flag/rate-limit) I should know about?

4) ENTRY POINT — AppRouter.presentLogin(from:) has exactly two callers: IntroductionContentViewController.didTapLogin ('Đăng nhập ngay' on the cold-start intro screen) and ProfileViewController.didTapSignIn ('Đăng nhập để tiếp tục' on the Cá nhân tab in guest mode). Which one should this test enter through — or both?

5) CREDENTIALS/ENV — config.yml names APP_TEST_USERNAME / APP_TEST_PASSWORD as the QA account env vars. Are those exported in the shell I'll run pytest in (I will not hardcode or commit any value), and which backend environment is the simulator pointed at right now (staging?)

1) SUCCESS — what exactly counts as 'ended up on the home screen' for this test to pass? 2) FAILURE — what does a real failure of this flow look like, i.e. what would the app do with a bad QA password? The doc (5.1 / AC-2) says a modal titled 'Tai khoan khong hop le' with a 'Dong' button and then a bounce back to the Introduction screen. The code shows LoginView.showError() setting an inline errorLabel with 'Sai ten dang nhap hoac mat khau' and staying on the login screen. Which is the expected behaviour I should hold the app to?

3) BLOCKERS — what could stop this test that is not the feature being broken? Things I already spotted and will confirm live: IntroductionContentViewController.viewDidLoad calls NotificationPermission.requestIfNeeded() (a system notification prompt on every launch, since reset_app_data: always), and LoginView keeps btnLogin disabled until the terms checkbox is ticked, with the terms link opening a WKWebView. Anything else I should plan for - OTP/2FA on the QA account, staging backend state, feature flags, rate limits? And is the account in APP_TEST_USERNAME/APP_TEST_PASSWORD valid on the environment the booted simulator points at?

4) ENTRY POINT — AppRouter.presentLogin is called from two places: IntroductionContentViewController.didTapLogin (cold start, the 'Dang nhap ngay' button on the intro screen) and ProfileViewController.didTapSignIn (guest browsing, 'Dang nhap de tiep tuc' in the Profile tab).
